findings.description: slugify() uses an inline uncompiled re.sub(r"-{2,}", ...) while the rest of the module pre-compiles its regexes at module scope (lines 6-7); CPython's internal re._cache mostly absorbs the cost, so this is a per-call cache-lookup micro-inefficiency and a consistency wart rather than a hot-loop problem — hoist it to a module-level compiled pattern.

indent_block() calls line.strip() once per line purely to test emptiness and then discards the stripped result (keeping the original line), allocating a throwaway string per line on large inputs; `if line and not line.isspace()` short-circuits and avoids the allocation, though the saving is marginal.
